decision_question: Should we invest in or develop a strategy around nuclear fusion energy, and if so, when and in which segments?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve grid-connected electricity generation before 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Determines investment horizon and need for interim technologies.
needed_evidence: Published timelines from major fusion projects (ITER, SPARC, etc.) and independent assessments.

hypotheses.hypothesis: First-of-a-kind fusion plants will have LCOE > $100/MWh, making them uncompetitive with renewables + storage by 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Affects economic justification for early adoption.
needed_evidence: LCOE estimates from fusion developers and independent studies; renewable LCOE projections.
search_queries: levelized cost of electricity fusion vs solar

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private fusion funding will exceed $10 billion cumulatively by 2025, but less than 20% of startups will reach a working prototype.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Indicates market confidence and survival rate.
needed_evidence: Funding data from Crunchbase, PitchBook; progress reports from fusion companies.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Government policy support (e.g., tax credits, regulatory sandboxes) is necessary for fusion commercialization before 2040.
needed_evidence: Policy announcements from US, UK, Japan; fusion-specific legislation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion will first be commercialized in niche applications (e.g., industrial heat, marine propulsion) rather than baseload electricity.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Shifts market focus from power generation to specialized uses.
needed_evidence: Developer roadmaps targeting non-power applications; market size for industrial heat.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The fusion supply chain (e.g., tritium, superconducting magnets) will face bottlenecks that delay commercialization by 5+ years.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Identifies critical dependencies and investment opportunities.
needed_evidence: Tritium availability studies; magnet production capacity; supply chain risk assessments.

hypotheses.hypothesis: By 2030, fusion energy will attract more private investment than nuclear fission, but less than solar and wind.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Benchmarks fusion against competing clean energy technologies.
needed_evidence: Investment data for fusion, fission, solar, wind from 2020-2030 projections.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total addressable market (TAM) = Global electricity generation (kWh) × fusion penetration rate × price per kWh
search_queries: IEA World Energy Outlook 2024 fusion scenario
known_limitations: Penetration rate is highly uncertain; assumes fusion is cost-competitive.

methods.formula: Market size = Number of fusion plants × average capacity (MW) × capacity factor × price per MWh
variables: Number of plants (based on announced projects and realistic build rates)
preferred_sources: Company announcements (e.g., Commonwealth Fusion, TAE Technologies)
known_limitations: Few announced projects; many may not materialize.

methods.formula: Market size = (Number of potential customers) × (adoption rate at each stage) × (revenue per customer)
variables: Potential customers: utilities, industrial users, governments
Adoption stages: awareness, interest, trial, adoption
Revenue per customer: plant sale or PPA value
search_queries: utility interest in fusion energy survey
fusion energy early adopter case study
known_limitations: Adoption rates are speculative; limited data on customer intent.

methods.formula: Value pool = Sum of profits across value chain (components, construction, fuel, O&M, electricity sales)
known_limitations: Value chain not yet established; many costs are estimates.

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Quantify TAM/SAM before writing a growth claim.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market size forecast data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Cross-check revenue pools with physical or usage demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications demand volume official data
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer count users

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Estimate realistic conversion from demand to revenue.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications adoption rate penetration
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications price ARPU ASP

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Test whether the opportunity can earn attractive economics.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications cost CAPEX OPEX

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Check whether supply can serve demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications capacity project pipeline

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Validate capital formation and investor conviction.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications funding investment by company
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications regulation policy approval

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Separate market growth from player-level advantage.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market share competitive landscape

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Verify that adoption exists outside narrative claims.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer case order announcement

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Anchor the commercialization clock in dated evidence.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications milestone timeline commercialization

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE vs. Renewables + Storage (2025-2050)
required_metrics: Fusion LCOE ($/MWh) - central estimate and range
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use published LCOE values from Lazard and IRENA for renewables; for fusion, use ranges from FIA or developer estimates. If only two endpoints exist, interpolate using CAGR from public forecasts and mark as estimates.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Funding Timeline (2010-2024) by Source
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How has investment evolved and who is investing?
comparison_set: Private vs. government funding over time
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use FIA annual funding surveys for private investment; DOE budget documents for government. Ensure at least 4 annual data points; if gaps exist, use linear interpolation with source note.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Startup Survival Curve
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What fraction of startups will reach commercialization?
comparison_set: Bubble size = funding, x-axis = year founded, y-axis = stage (ordinal), color = status
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Include only companies with verifiable funding data. Stage must be based on public milestones. Exclude companies with no public information.

chart_data_needs.title: Key Fusion Projects and Their Milestones
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What is the realistic timeline for first power?
required_metrics: Milestone (e.g., first plasma, net energy, grid connection)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use only official project announcements or independent reviews. Mark delays with source. If a milestone is not publicly dated, exclude.

chart_data_needs.title: Tritium Supply-Demand Gap Forecast
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Will tritium availability limit fusion deployment?
required_metrics: Tritium supply (kg/year) from CANDU reactors and other sources
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use IAEA data for supply; demand based on fusion plant scenarios (e.g., 1 GW plant consumes ~56 kg/year). If only two points, use linear interpolation with source note.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion vs. Fission vs. Solar vs. Wind Investment (2020-2030)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How does fusion investment compare to other clean energy technologies?
required_metrics: Annual investment in nuclear fission ($ billion)
comparison_set: Fusion vs. fission vs. solar vs. wind
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use IEA and BNEF data for historical and projected values. For fusion, use FIA data. If projections are not available, use CAGR from recent trends and mark as estimates.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE Sensitivity to Key Variables
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which variables most affect fusion cost competitiveness?
required_metrics: Variable (capital cost, capacity factor, discount rate, fuel cost)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use published sensitivity ranges from credible sources. If only base case available, assume ±30% for capital cost and ±10% for capacity factor. Mark assumptions.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should we invest in or develop a strategy around nuclear fusion energy, and if so, when and in which segments?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).
